brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +52.1% since Feb 27 — surges past $108 after Iran rejects peace framework; Trump extends power plant deadline 10 days to Apr 6; IEA: largest supply disruption ever
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri): Brent $108.01/bbl, +5.66% on the day — Iran rejection of 15-point peace plan drives further surge. Trump extends power plant strike deadline 10 days to Apr 6. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market' — 8 mb/d supply plunge in March, Gulf production cut 10+ mb/d. Brent-WTI spread ~$13.53 reflects acute waterborne premium. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Fortune / TECHi / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +42.1% since Feb 27 — WTI rebounds to $94.48 on Iran rejection; gas steady at $3.98; Brent-WTI spread ~$13.53
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri): WTI $94.48, rebounding on Iran's rejection of peace framework. Gas at $3.98/gal national average (AAA) — up $1.00 in one month from $2.98. Brent-WTI spread ~$13.53. California $5.79-$5.82. Oklahoma cheapest at $3.24-$3.25. Diesel $5.10. USPS 8% fuel surcharge. Source: NYMEX / AAA / AP.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.00 / +33.6% since Feb 27 — $3.98 AAA avg — up $1.00 in one month — highest since Aug 2022 — CA $5.79-$5.82
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 26: $3.981/gal (+33.6% since war began). Up $1.00 in one month — highest since August 2022. California $5.79-$5.82 (highest). Oklahoma $3.24-$3.25 (cheapest). Diesel $5.10. USPS imposed 8% fuel surcharge. AP-NORC: 45% extremely/very concerned about gas affordability (up from 30% pre-war). 59% say war 'gone too far.' Source: AAA / AP-NORC.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -402 pts / -5.8% — S&P falls to 6,477 on Iran war uncertainty; VIX 27.44; futures +0.39% on deadline extension
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 26 close: 6,477.16, -1.74% on the day. Mar 27 futures +0.39% on Trump extending power plant deadline to Apr 6. VIX 27.44. S&P -5.8% from pre-war. Defense: RTX +22.1%, LMT +19.4% MTD. Energy: XOM +16.4% MTD. Airlines/cruise battered. Source: NYSE / AP / Bloomberg.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 10,000+ targets — 8,000+ sorties — 82nd Airborne deploying — Trump extends deadline to Apr 6 — G7 fractures — $200B supplemental
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 28: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets, 8,000+ combat sorties. Cooper confirmed 10,000+ targets struck. 82nd Airborne deploying to CENTCOM. Trump extends power plant strike deadline 10 days to Apr 6. G7 fractures over continued operations. Pentagon $200B supplemental before Congress. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Replenishment $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (28 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$32.8-33.3B — $200B supplemental proposed
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 28: ~$32.8-33.3B. Pentagon $200B supplemental before Congress. Speaker Johnson: 'probably' in reconciliation bill. Trump expects war 4-6 weeks. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Replenishment: $3.1B+ (~$758M/day). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 10,000+ US targets — 8,000+ combat sorties — IDF: 1,000+ weapons production targets — 92% of large naval vessels destroyed — 2/3 arms manufacturing
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 27): 10,000+ US targets, 8,000+ combat sorties — Cooper confirmed 10,000+ targets struck. IDF overnight blitz on Tehran/Kermanshah/Dezful ~70 bombs targeting weapons production. 92% of large naval vessels destroyed. Two-thirds of Iran's arms manufacturing destroyed. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~29,000+ through Day 28. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / NPR / Al Jazeera.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 US KIA total — Sgt. Pennington (Glendale, KY) latest — IDF Sgt. Volansky killed in Lebanon — 290 WIA (255 RTD, 10 seriously wounded)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 US KIA total including Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky — latest announced. IDF Sgt. Volansky killed in southern Lebanon (Mar 27). 290 WIA (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded). 82nd Airborne (~2,000 paratroopers) deploying to CENTCOM — Maj. Gen. Tegtmeier commanding. Source: Department of War / CENTCOM / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 290 total — 255 returned to duty — 10 seriously wounded — 50,000+ deployed + 2,000 82nd ABN deploying
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 290 WIA (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded). 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR plus ~2,000 82nd Airborne paratroopers deploying. Source: Department of War / CENTCOM / Pentagon.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 6,530 (5,890 military, 640 civilian) — HRANA: 3,291 (1,455 civilian, 217 children) — Iran govt: 1,937 — significant source discrepancy
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Hengaw (Mar 27): 6,530 total (5,890 military, 640 civilian). HRANA: 3,291 documented (1,455 civilian, 217 children). Iran govt: 1,937. Iranian Red Crescent: 1,400+ killed, 18,000+ civilians injured, 204 children. Tehran alone: 503 killed, 6,848 injured. Health Ministry: 300 health facilities damaged, 210+ children. IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed Mar 26. Navy lost 43+ warships, 80% air defenses destroyed. IDF overnight blitz on Tehran/Kermanshah/Dezful ~70 bombs. Source: Hengaw / HRANA / Red Crescent / Iran Health Ministry.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,116 killed — 3,229 wounded — 830,000 displaced (~20% pop) — 121 children — 42 health workers — IDF Sgt. Volansky killed — 162nd Div toward Litani
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,116 killed, 3,229 wounded. 830,000+ displaced (~20% of population). 121 children killed. 42 health workers killed. IDF Sgt. Volansky killed in southern Lebanon. 162nd Division advancing toward Litani River. Hezbollah chief Qassem rejected truce: 'negotiations under fire amounts to imposing surrender.' Israel expanding buffer zone (~30km). Hezbollah/Amal ministers boycotted Lebanese cabinet over Iran ambassador expulsion. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / Times of Israel / Al Jazeera.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.2M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 830K+ — UNHCR $454.2M appeal — IFRC CHF 40M — Philippines declares state of emergency
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.2M+ total displaced. Iran: up to 3.2M (UNHCR). Lebanon: 830,000+ (~20% of pop). UNHCR $454.2M Inter-Agency Refugee Response Plan. IFRC CHF 40M appeal. Philippines declares state of emergency over displaced workers. 24,600 Afghans returned from Iran. 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. 3,000+ vessels idled. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IFRC / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ seafarers — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 210+ (Health Min) / HRANA 217 / Red Crescent 204 + Lebanon 118+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren)
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. HRANA: 217 children confirmed. Red Crescent: 204 children. Amnesty verified Minab school: 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) — US Tomahawk fragments confirmed — outdated DIA intel. UN investigation initiated Mar 17, ongoing. Lebanon: 118+ children (UNICEF). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. UNICEF: 2,100+ children killed or injured across region. Source: UNICEF / Amnesty / HRANA / Red Crescent / Iran Health Min.
